rows.notes: Page title/section header, centered at top and contains no monetary figures. Wording legible though final vowel uncertain; taken as 'solata varia.'

rows.description: May 11. Pd Mr Thompson former fill in full
rows.notes: Date and name clear. Amount aligned to the pounds column and reads '2' in pounds, with shillings and pence columns blank/zero. Vertical ruling for pounds unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment made to Mr Thompson, settled in full.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment made to Mr Thompson, settled in full, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Date and description legible. Amount columns clearly show '17' in pounds and '4' in shillings. Pence column appears zero/blank.
rows.english_description: Payment to Colley Clayton settled in full.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Colley Clayton settled in full, amounting to £17 4s 0d.

rows.notes: Description clear. Pounds column shows '11', shillings and pence columns blank/zero and alignment is consistent with other rows.
rows.english_description: Payment of Molineux's mess (boarding/mess) bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Molineux's mess bill, amounting to £11 0s 0d.

rows.description: 19. Mr Williams vice J. Newington for Michl: 1766.
rows.notes: Long description but amounts are clear: '10' in the pounds column, with shillings/pence blank. Alignment consistent with vertical guides.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Williams in the place of J. Newington for Michaelmas 1766.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Williams in the place of J. Newington for Michaelmas 1766, amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.description: July 8. Mr Newbery's yrs salary for motion due this Midsumer
rows.notes: Description partly faded/uncertain in a couple of letters ('motion' / 'Midsumer' uncertain) but amount '15' in pounds column is clear and aligns with ruling. Chosen 15-0-0.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Newbery's year's salary due at Midsummer.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Newbery's year's salary due at Midsummer, amounting to £15 0s 0d.

rows.description: Aug 5. Pd Geeler in part of his Bill
rows.notes: Date and name legible; pounds column clearly '25'. Shillings and pence blank/zero. Vertical alignment strong.
rows.english_description: Part payment to Geeler on account of his bill.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Part payment to Geeler on account of his bill, amounting to £25 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Second 'in part' payment on same date block; amount looks identical to previous row and '25' in pounds column is clear. Description letters somewhat abbreviated but identifiable as glazier & plumber.
rows.english_description: Part payment to the glazier and plumber for their work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Part payment to the glazier and plumber for their work, amounting to £25 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Surname/read as 'Joyce' (could be 'Jouge' or similar); amount columns read as 5 pounds, 11 shillings, 4 pence. Some ink smearing around the pence digit makes exact pence slightly uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment of Joyce the silversmith's bill (works for college plate or silver).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Joyce the silversmith's bill, amounting to £5 11s 4d.

rows.notes: Description heavily abbreviated/faded (read as 'millechiy' but uncertain). Amount columns appear to read '2' pounds and '11' shillings; pence column looks blank/zero. Column boundaries faint here so reading of shillings is moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Unclear — possible transcription error in the bill description.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Unclear — possible transcription error in the bill description; amount recorded £2 11s 0d.

rows.notes: Description interpreted as 'Pavior Smithy Bill' (could be 'Pavior'/'Paviour'). Pence area shows '10' with a small trailing loop resembling 'ob' (halfpenny); interpreted as 10½d. Ink faint on fraction, so confidence moderate-low.
rows.english_description: Payment of the pavior and smithy bill for paving/smith work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of the pavior and smithy bill, amounting to £7 8s 10½d.

rows.description: 11. Pd Mr Langford Prov: of Bail: Quit Rent Mich: 1766.
rows.notes: Date and name readable. Pound column blank (0). Shillings read as '12' and pence '6' with clear alignment. Some letters in description slightly abbreviated but meaning clear.
rows.english_description: Quit rent paid to Mr Langford, Provost of Balliol, for Michaelmas 1766.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quit rent paid to Mr Langford, Provost of Balliol, for Michaelmas 1766, amounting to £0 12s 6d.

rows.description: 13. Pd for Bell Rope & Lamp Baskets
rows.notes: Description legible as 'Bell Rope & Lamp Baskets'. Amount columns aligned; reads 0 pounds, 5 shillings, 6 pence. No fraction mark visible.
rows.english_description: Payment for bell rope and lamp baskets (purchase or repair of fixtures).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for bell rope and lamp baskets, amounting to £0 5s 6d.

rows.notes: Clear short description. Amount shown as 0 pounds, 3 shillings, 0 pence; alignment with shillings column clear.
rows.english_description: Payment of a scavenger's bill for cleaning/removal work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of a scavenger's bill for cleaning/removal work, amounting to £0 3s 0d.

rows.description: Octr 11. Pd Dr Kennicott & the Subscriptions
rows.notes: Date and recipient clear. Amount reads '5' pounds and '5' shillings. Pence column blank. Ink and column alignment are good here.
rows.english_description: Payment to Dr Kennicott and payment of subscriptions (likely to a publication or society).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Dr Kennicott and subscriptions, amounting to £5 5s 0d.

rows.description: 16. Ch. Curtis for Battle Books Ink & Paper
rows.notes: Description and small supplies list legible. Amount clearly reads 0 pounds, 17 shillings, 6 pence; digits crisp and column boundaries clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Ch. Curtis for books, ink and paper (stationery and account/book supplies).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Ch. Curtis for books, ink and paper, amounting to £0 17s 6d.

rows.description: Back Garden & Inder(?) Expenses to ye Rector
rows.notes: Description partly unclear ('Inder' uncertain). Amount reads '2' pounds and '4' shillings clearly. Pence blank. Used alignment with neighboring rows to confirm pounds and shillings.
rows.english_description: Reimbursement to the Rector for back-garden and indoor expenses.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Reimbursement to the Rector for back-garden and indoor expenses, amounting to £2 4s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads 1 pound, 18 shillings and 11 pence, with a small trailing mark after the pence that resembles a halfpenny sign ('ob'). The fraction mark is faint; chosen halfpenny (0.5). Column boundaries slightly less distinct here.
rows.english_description: Payment of procuration (ecclesiastical dues) owing from former years (arrears).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of procuration dues from former years, amounting to £1 18s 11½d.

rows.notes: Short entry 'ditto' for another item of prior year; amount reads 7 shillings exactly. Columns aligned and legible.
rows.english_description: Payment of synodal dues for a former year (ecclesiastical arrear).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of synodal dues for a former year, amounting to £0 7s 0d.

rows.description: 30. Pd George Lambert for Common Room wine
rows.notes: Name (George Lambert) and purpose (Common Room wine) legible. Amount clearly 2-10-6 with crisp digits and good column alignment.
rows.english_description: Payment to George Lambert for common-room wine.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to George Lambert for common-room wine, amounting to £2 10s 6d.

rows.description: Pd John Hart for removing Rubbish
rows.notes: Short description clear. Amount reads 0-8-0. Shillings digit '8' is distinct and pence column blank.
rows.english_description: Payment to John Hart for removing rubbish.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to John Hart for removing rubbish, amounting to £0 8s 0d.

rows.notes: Section total (receipts). Pounds '922' is clearly written; pence shows '3' with a clear '¾' (three-quarters) mark. Vertical alignment with totals column is unambiguous.
Payment total. Pounds '879' and shillings '7' clear. Pence reads '6' with a '¾' after it (three-quarters of a penny). The fraction mark is slightly smudged but recognizable.
Adjustment/ balancing line. '42-12-9' clearly written as an accounting adjustment. The Latin/abbreviated wording 'A/c Solved' and 'Computatus' are legible though ornate.
Short dated note with no monetary amount. Text 'per die 2d Novr 1769' legible; interpreted as a date/reference (no columns used).
rows.english_description: Dated notation: 2 November 1769.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Dated notation: 2 November 1769; no amount recorded.

rows.notes: Latin heading introducing signatures/attestation. Handwriting ornate and partly faded; no monetary figures present. Exact Latin spelling uncertain but purpose clear.

rows.description: Geras Webber Rectorum. Tho: Bray Subrectorem. T. Newbery Decan. Gul: Tomkin S.T.P.B. J. ?
rows.notes: Signature block with several names. Individual names mostly legible (e.g. 'Webber', 'Bray', 'Newbery', 'Tomkin'); final signature is hard to read. No monetary amounts. Grouped into single row to capture the attestation block.
rows.section_header: Geras Webber Rectorum. Tho: Bray Subrectorem. T. Newbery Decan. Gul: Tomkin S.T.P.B. J. ?
